title: The Red Colosseum
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Red Colosseum}}__NOTOC__
[[File:Colosseum Icon.png|400px|link=]]
'''The Red Colosseum'''
{{Quote|As soon as you enter the '''Red Colosseum''', you are no longer yourself; you are only entertainment for forces beyond the comprehension of any human.|A survivor of the '''Red Colosseum'''|color=#d4d4d4}}
Description{{Underline|000000}} '''The Red Colosseum'''Sometimes simply called '''The Colosseum''' for convenience. owes its name to the giant colosseum made of red and black stone that it constitutes, as well as the predominantly red cosmos above it. Unlike Earth's Colosseum, this one is considerably larger, perfectly circular, and remains unspoiled by time. The architecture is also slightly different, being even more ruthless and vicious in appearance and featuring abundant spire-like spikes that stretch in various upward directions from the roof and walls alike. A layer of black sand covers the arena's stone floor. Assumed to be unimaginably vast in size, the red cosmos above — and presumably all around — the colosseum contains patches of orange, yellow, and black, as well as countless distant white stars. This cosmos is believed to be the source of the level's substantial illumination. Only two wanderers can be in the '''Red Colosseum''' at once, and they are always transported there at the exact same time. As soon as they arrive, immensely powerful bloodlust and rage that completely overrides one's humanity will immediately overcome them bothPrevious usage of [[Object 1]], no matter how recent, cannot prevent this at all., leading them to fight to the death. According to every available record, no one has been able to overcome these urges and at least one wanderer has died each time, though cases of neither individual coming out of the level have very rarely been reported. Nonetheless, provided one of the fighters survives the battle and their opponent is either killed or somehow dies from other causes, the survivor will be transported out of the '''Red Colosseum''', whereupon the violent urges caused by the level will abruptly disappear. In addition to the intense bloodlust, everyone who is sent to the level also undergoes temporary, unnatural physical changes; thus, these altered wanderers are classified as entities in their own right. Entities{{Underline|000000}} No entities are present within the '''Red Colosseum''' save for the individuals, whom are referred to as 'Gladiators', who battle in the level. Gladiators
Upon arrival, wanderers in the '''Red Colosseum''' will begin rapidly growing extremely tough, grey, chitin-like armor from their bodies. This process takes mere seconds, after which the chitin will somewhat resemble full-body gladiatorial armor. The chitin will also form long blades that cover and extend from the hands, with the hands shrinking and morphing to suit the alteration. This leaves the Gladiators unable to hold, let alone wield, anything else, although the blades are already stronger than any other known type of blade. Moreover, Gladiators will instantly experience an increase in strength, a high surge of adrenaline, and partially diminished pain, lessening yet not completely nullifying the otherwise debilitating agony of the chitin growing from their skin. The Gladiators will go to battle as soon as their transformations are complete. They primarily use their chitin blades to attack, though they may also use other, less effective methods such as headbutting and kicking. As a result of the sheer durability of their armor, the fights often last for a considerable amount of time, though survivors typically do not clearly recall details such as time spans. Despite their feral state, Gladiators typically still retain at least some of their fighting skills and sometimes deliberately aim for the tiny gaps in their opponents' armor, often around the eyes. If a Gladiator manages to penetrate or slice off a sizeable portion of the other's armor, they normally end up winning the battle due to the ease of piercing the vulnerable flesh underneath. No matter how wounded they are, Gladiators will never give up until they have slain their opponent. Once the fight is over, their armor will shrink and disappear back into their skin, their hands will return to regular shape, and their increased strength, adrenaline, and lessened pain reception will immediately vanish along with their anomalous bloodlust. Colonies & Outposts{{Underline|000000}} As one can imagine, colonies and outposts are unable to exist in the '''Red Colosseum'''. Entrances & Exits{{Underline|000000}} Entrances
Wanderers are transported to the Red Colosseum seemingly randomlyExcept, of course, for the fact that one is always transported at the same time as the other., although unconfirmed data indicates that one is more likely to enter the level if they have recently shed another's blood or experienced a high surge of bloodthirst. Exits
Seconds after the other wanderer dies, and provided the surviving individual lives, the survivor will be transported to a random level.

{{Quote|I escaped the accursed '''Colosseum''', but my conscience feels permanently tainted. How can I live with the knowledge that I not only willingly, but gleefully, took another's life?|Another survivor of the '''Red Colosseum'''|color=#dedede}}
